title: plocama kandaharensis
intro: Plocama kandaharensis is a type of shrub that belongs to the Rutaceae family, native to the Kandahar region in Afghanistan. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like limonene, beta-pinene, and beta-caryophyllene, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Plocama kandaharensis include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, improve digestion, and reduce fever, among other uses. Plocama kandaharensis is typically prepared as essential oil through steam distillation, which can be used in aromatherapy, or as a herbal tea made from its leaves and flowers.
